points.text: Common symptoms of COVID-19 include coughing, fever, and loss of smell and taste.
Less common symptoms are headaches, nasal congestion, runny nose, muscle pain, sore throat, diarrhea, and eye irritation.
In moderate to severe cases, breathing difficulties may occur.
Symptoms can begin one to 14 days after exposure to the virus.
Three symptom clusters are identified: respiratory, musculoskeletal, and digestive symptoms.
COVID-19 symptoms can include congestion, runny nose, or sore throat.
Common symptoms are dry cough, shortness of breath, and loss of taste or smell.
Extreme tiredness, known as fatigue, is a symptom of COVID-19.
Digestive symptoms such as upset stomach, vomiting, or diarrhea may occur.
Fever or chills, headaches, and body or muscle aches are symptoms of COVID-19.
Common symptoms include fever, cough, and tiredness.
Other symptoms can include shortness of breath, chest pain, muscle aches, headache, and loss of taste or smell.
Symptoms may appear 2 to 14 days after exposure and range from mild to severe.
Infants may have difficulty feeding as a symptom.
Symptoms requiring urgent attention include difficulty breathing, blue lips, chest pain, confusion, and inability to drink.
